title: Innovative Citrusy Japanese Provola Tamagoyaki
description: A kid-friendly Japanese dish featuring provola, citrus, and a bouncy texture, perfect for a snack or light meal. This vegan-friendly version uses plant-based ingredients to replicate the traditional tamagoyaki.
instructions: In a blender, combine soy milk, citrus juice, rice vinegar, sugar, salt, kombu dashi powder, and turmeric, and blend until smooth.
In a bowl, whisk together the blended mixture and sliced provolone cheese alternative until well combined.
Heat a tamagoyaki pan over medium heat and brush with a small amount of oil.
Pour a small amount of the mixture into the pan and tilt to evenly coat the bottom.
Use a spatula to gently lift and fold the edges of the tamagoyaki towards the center.
Repeat the process until all the mixture is used up, creating multiple layers.
Steam the tamagoyaki for 5 minutes to cook through and create a bouncy texture.
Remove from heat and let cool before slicing and serving.
